$comment: Tier-2 DB-state fixture spec for the `plugin_config` repo's
`upsertForUserPlugin` method (Phase-2), in the MINTED-VALUES (remap) form.
One committed file drives BOTH sides of the differential:
- the v4 oracle (build-plugin-config-upsert-fixture.ts +
plugin-config-upsert-tier2.ts) and
- the Rust port (quilltap-harness plugin_config_upsert tier-2 test).
WHAT THIS BANKS: v4's `upsertForUserPlugin(userId, pluginName, config)`:
- find existing by (userId, pluginName);
- if found -> MERGE `{ ...existing.config, ...config }` then
`update(existing.id, {config: merged})` (id + createdAt preserved,
updatedAt minted);
- else -> `create({userId, pluginName, config})` (mints id + both
timestamps; `enabled` is never set -> SQL NULL throughout).
MINTED-VALUES / REMAP FORM: the upsert ops pin NOTHING. v4 and the Rust port
independently mint random UUIDs (on the create path) and wall-clock
timestamps (createdAt on create, updatedAt on every op). The harness
reconciles the two dumps by normalizing only the nondeterministic fields:
first-seen `id` remap (rows sorted by the natural key `pluginName`, an input)
+ createdAt/updatedAt placeholder. Everything else (userId, pluginName,
config, enabled) is diffed exactly. The createdAt==updatedAt invariant is NOT
asserted here: the UPDATE path re-mints updatedAt while preserving createdAt,
so the two diverge by design.
OPEN-JSON MERGE CONSTRAINT (tracked deferred seam #5 in phase-2-onramp.md):
every stored config -- INCLUDING every MERGE result -- is {} or a SINGLE-KEY
object, so v4's JSON.stringify (insertion order) and Rust's serde_json::Value
(sorted keys) serialize byte-identically. The merge ops are designed so the
existing and new config share the SAME single key (the merge overwrites the
value -> still single-key), or an empty existing merges with a single-key
new. A 2+-key merge result would diverge -- see the module header in
plugin_config.rs.
CORPUS (>=4 upsert ops, both paths exercised, each final row's pluginName
distinct):
op[0] upsert (AA, qtap-plugin-curl, {"threshold":0.9}) -> UPDATE of seed
row 1: merge {threshold:0.5} with {threshold:0.9} -> {threshold:0.9}
(same single key, value overwritten). enabled stays true (untouched).
op[1] upsert (AA, qtap-plugin-search, {"maxResults":0.25}) -> CREATE of a
minted-id row. enabled never set -> SQL NULL.
op[2] upsert (AA, qtap-plugin-search, {"maxResults":0.75}) -> UPDATE of the
minted-id row from op[1]: merge {maxResults:0.25} with
{maxResults:0.75} -> {maxResults:0.75} (same single key, overwritten).
op[3] upsert (AA, qtap-plugin-fresh, {}) -> CREATE of a minted-id row with
an empty {} config. enabled -> SQL NULL.
Final rows (4, distinct pluginNames): curl, fresh, search, weather (seed 2,
untouched). enabled is SQL NULL on the three created/upserted rows; true on
curl (seed); NULL on weather (seed omitted it).
SAFETY: testPepperBase64 is a THROWAWAY 32-byte key, the same one the
folders / tags / plugin_config fixtures use. It is NOT the real Friday pepper
and is safe to commit.

ops.comment: UPDATE seed row 1 via merge: {threshold:0.5} <- {threshold:0.9} -> {threshold:0.9}. Same single key, value overwritten -> still single-key. enabled (true) untouched. updatedAt re-minted, createdAt preserved.
CREATE a minted-id row (no existing (userId, pluginName)). Single-key config. enabled never set -> SQL NULL.
UPDATE the minted-id row from op[1] via merge: {maxResults:0.25} <- {maxResults:0.75} -> {maxResults:0.75}. Same single key, overwritten -> still single-key.
CREATE a minted-id row with an empty {} config. enabled -> SQL NULL.
